title: Sticky Soy Chicken & Chilli Crisp Tomatoes
summary: If there’s one chicken recipe I think would go down well with everyone (including picky eaters), it’s this one. Sticky soy chicken (basically teriyaki) with chilli crisp tomato salad on top of rice. The juices trickle into the rice… woooow. Perfect dinner.

ingredients.label: 8 Chicken Thighs, skin on, bone in
ingredients.name: Skin-On Chicken Thigh

ingredients.label: 8 Chicken Thighs, skin on, bone in
ingredients.name: Chicken

method.description: Heat the oven to 200°C. Pat the chicken dry and season with salt. Place the chicken, skin-side down, into an ovenproof frying pan. Set the heat to medium-high and cook for 8-12 mins until the skin turns golden and the chicken can easily flip over.

Meanwhile, stir the soy sauce, sugar, rice vinegar and water together in a bowl. Once the chicken skin is crispy, turn the thighs skin-side up and pour the sauce into the pan. Transfer to the oven and roast for 20-25 mins, until cooked through.
Cook the rice following packet instructions.
Remove the chicken from the oven and place the pan straight over a medium heat to reduce the sauce. It should glaze the chicken nicely.
Meanwhile, for the salad, trim and thinly slice the spring onions, then chop the tomatoes into bite-sized chunks.
Put the chilli oil into a large bowl and stir through the rice vinegar. Season with salt, then add the tomatoes and spring onion. Toss well.
Share the rice between bowls, then top with the chicken and tomato salad. Drizzle the pan juices over the chicken.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
